chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.D- 918 of 2024. DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE Mr,Justice Muhammad Saleem Jessar. Mr.Justi Adnan-ul-Karim on. Mr. Ghulam Murtaza Korai, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Aslam Jatoi, Asstt: Attorney General a/w l.O SIP Wajid Ali Darban, FlRl/Cyber Crime. ADNAN-UL-KARIM MEMON-J.:- A father has petitioned the court, requesting it compel the FIA Cyber Crime Sukkur to finalize its investigation (Inquiry No. 46/2023) into the online harassment of his daughter. Zoya Shaikh. Ms Zoya was targeted with a fake Facebook profile that posted edited, explicit images, causing severe distress since her marriage to her cousin. Despite filing a complaint with the FIA in 2023, and the FIA confirming the inquiry's registration. the petitioner alleges inaction' He claims the FIA has neglected its duty to identify the perpetrator, resulting in ongoing mental anguish for his family. He seeks a court order directing the FIA to complete the investigation and prosecute the responsible individual. He prayed for allowing the petition. o and released due to insufficient evidence. Forensic analysis of phones d no evidence of the fake profile or related content The tigation was co^ar.tJJ transparently, but no evidence q IA asserts the.ln l i 1. For orders on office objections as flag A. 2.For orders on M.A No. 361812024. 3.For hearinq of main case. 25.02.2025 ORDER. 2. Learned DAG has submitted that The FIA investigation into the fake Facebook profile harassing Zoya Shaikh revealed the profile was linked to a mobile number registered to Muhammad Azeem.

For orders on office objections as flag A. 2.For orders on M.A No. 361812024. 3.For hearinq of main case. 25.02.2025 ORDER. 2. Learned DAG has submitted that The FIA investigation into the fake Facebook profile harassing Zoya Shaikh revealed the profile was linked to a mobile number registered to Muhammad Azeem. He denied involvement, claiming his number was misused. His phone was seized and sent for forensic analysis. Call Detail Records (CDRs) linked the number to Ishaque Shaikfu who albo denied involvement, stating his mother's SIM was lost. His phone was also seized for forensic analysis. Other suspects were supports the complainant's claims. The fake profile has been closed. The FIA requests the court to direct the petitioner to provide further evidence and dismiss the petition. 3. With the FIA finding no incriminating evidence against the alleged culprits, this petition is dismissed. The FIA is to close the investigation but may prosecute if new evidence arises. I
